title: Marbella Dolphin Watching Boat Hire: Costs and Tips 2026
html:
Marbella offers superb dolphin watching along the Costa del Sol, with trips departing from Puerto Banús or Marbella Marina for around €60, €200 per person on shared tours. Book a private boat through BoatHire24 for a more exclusive experience spotting common and striped dolphins in the Alboran Sea.

Where to set off in Marbella?

Most dolphin watching trips leave from Puerto Banús, Marbella Marina, or nearby Cabopino Port. These harbours offer easy access to the dolphin-rich waters off the coast. Other departure points include La Duquesa Marina to the west and the sheltered bay of Estepona. Many skippers head towards the Cabo de Gata marine reserve or the Strait of Gibraltar, where pods of up to 50 dolphins can be seen. You can yacht charter in Marbella for your chosen dates.

How much does a dolphin watching boat cost in Marbella?

A shared dolphin watching tour costs from €60 per person, while a private motorboat starts around €400 for a half-day. For a more luxurious experience, a crewed catamaran or a yacht charter in Marbella may cost €1,500, €3,000 for a full-day trip. Prices vary by season and vessel size. You can compare verified boat listings on the BoatHire24 platform to find the best deal, including a dolphin-watching-specific option that includes photography equipment: deep inside the orientation section, a typical yacht charter in Marbella might cost €800, €1,200 for a half-day private hire with a skipper. One option worth a look is the Sunseeker 63 charter page.

Which boat to rent for dolphin watching in Marbella?

  1. RIB or rigid inflatable, fast and nimble, perfect for chasing dolphin pods; carries up to 8 guests; from €350 for a half-day.
  2. Motor yacht (e.g., Sunseeker 63), comfortable and stable, ideal for groups or families; carries up to 10 guests; from €900 for a half-day. See the Sunseeker 63 charter page for details.
  3. Catamaran (e.g., Lagoon 46), spacious decks and steady ride; carries up to 12 guests; from €1,200 for a half-day.
  4. Luxury motor yacht (e.g., Azimut 66), full crew and premium amenities; carries up to 12 guests; from €2,000 for a full-day.

Cost breakdown for dolphin watching boat hire in Marbella

  • Shared dolphin tour: €60, €100 per person, typically 2-3 hours, includes guide and sometimes refreshments.
  • Small motorboat (private, half-day): €400, €700 for up to 6 guests, no licence required with a skipper.
  • Catamaran (private, full-day): €1,200, €2,000 for up to 12 guests, includes crew and lunch.
  • Luxury crewed yacht (private, full-day): €2,500, €5,000 for up to 12 guests, all-inclusive.

Season and licence requirements for Marbella boat rental

The best months for dolphin watching are May to October, with peak sightings in July and August when Levante winds are light. The Alboran Sea hosts resident pods of bottlenose and striped dolphins year-round. For boat hire, if you rent without a skipper, you may need a Spanish recreational licence or an International Certificate of Competence; the operator will confirm exact requirements. Always check the conditions with your charter company. If you own a boat, you can become a host and earn from spare days.

Early morning trips in summer (departing 8, 9 am) often encounter the calmest seas and most active dolphin feeding, especially near the Cabo Pino reef.

At a glance

  • Price band: €60, €5,000+ per trip
  • Peak months: July and August
  • Trip length: 2, 8 hours
  • Guest capacity: 6, 12 persons
  • Currency: EUR
  • Distance to dolphin grounds: 2, 15 nautical miles offshore

Questions about dolphin watching boat hire in Marbella

Do I need a licence to rent a boat in Marbella for dolphin watching?

If you rent with a skipper, no licence is required; if you self-drive, you typically need a Spanish licence or ICC, but the operator will specify on the listing.

What types of dolphins can I see off Marbella?

You are most likely to see common dolphins and striped dolphins, and occasionally bottlenose dolphins, especially in the waters between Marbella and the Cabo de Gata.

Can I take my family on a dolphin watching boat in Marbella?

Yes, many boats are family-friendly, with life jackets for children and comfortable seating; private charters can be tailored to your group size.

What is the best time of day for dolphin watching in Marbella?

Early morning (around 8, 10 am) is generally best because the sea is calm and dolphins feed actively, but sunset trips are also popular for their light.

Whether you are a first-time visitor or a seasoned sailor, renting a boat in Marbella through BoatHire24 gives you access to verified boats at competitive prices. If you own a boat in Marbella, you can also become a host and earn income by listing your vessel. BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Marbella or list your own.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Marbella and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
